result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deb       Creates .deb from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.
  appimage  Creates AppImage from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deb       Creates .deb from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.
  appimage  Creates AppImage from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates AppImage from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console appimage [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The input directory to create the package from
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Output file (.AppImage)
  --application-name <application-name>                                                                         Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information

Commands:
  appdir       Creates an AppDir from a directory (does not package an .AppImage). For .NET apps, pass the publish directory.
  from-appdir  Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Creates AppImage from a directory with the contents. Everything is inferred. For .NET applications, this is usually the "publish" directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console appimage [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The input directory to create the package from
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Output file (.AppImage)
  --application-name <application-name>                                                                         Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information

Commands:
  appdir       Creates an AppDir from a directory (does not package an .AppImage). For .NET apps, pass the publish directory.
  from-appdir  Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates an AppDir from a directory (does not package an .AppImage). For .NET apps, pass the publish directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console appimage appdir [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The input directory (publish output)
  --output-dir <output-dir> (REQUIRED)                                                                          Destination directory for the AppDir
  --application-name <application-name>                                                                         Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information

  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console appimage from-appdir [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The AppDir directory to package
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Output .AppImage file
  --executable-relative-path <executable-relative-path>                                                         Executable inside the AppDir (relative), e.g., usr/bin/MyApp
  --application-name <application-name>                                                                         Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Console appimage from-appdir [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The AppDir directory to package
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Output .AppImage file
  --executable-relative-path <executable-relative-path>                                                         Executable inside the AppDir (relative), e.g., usr/bin/MyApp
  --application-name <application-name>                                                                         Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
